tier_rationale: This updates multiple guide documents to clarify language policy for structured JSON artifacts. It does not modify runtime product code.
request_summary: Clarify that Task Spec JSON and task ledger JSONL can be written in English, while session logs and reports remain Korean.
goal: Update the authoritative guide documents so future agents use English for structured JSON artifacts when practical and Korean only for user-facing session logs and reports.

positive: Keep logs/sessions/*.md and reports/*.md as Korean user-facing documents.
Preserve existing enum and schema fields in English.
negative: Do not change runtime product code.
Do not rewrite historical task ledgers or specs.
Do not include secrets, tokens, passwords, or PII.

success_criteria: docs/schemas/output-formats.md explicitly separates JSON/JSONL artifact language from session/report language.
docs/operations/work-history-policy.md states task ledgers may use English for structured fields and details.
docs/schemas/task-spec.md states Task Spec JSON may be written in English.

context.relevant_history: The previous TASK-20260427-084 used Korean in JSON and JSONL artifacts even though the current rules only require Korean for session logs and final reports.
